# clang trunk: fatal error "Broken module found" in Verifier due to oversized struct with huge flexible array member

**Author:** Theaamadu
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173676
**Status:** Open
**Labels:** clang:codegen, crash, generated by fuzzer

## Body

## Compiler Explorer: https://godbolt.org/z/9v6KvK6bx

- clang start crashing on `Trunk`
- generated by fuzzer

## Console
```console
fatal error: error in backend: Broken module found, compilation aborted!
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.
Stack dump:
0.	Program arguments: /opt/compiler-explorer/clang-assertions-trunk/bin/clang -g -o /app/output.s -mllvm --x86-asm-syntax=intel -fno-verbose-asm -S --gcc-toolchain=/opt/compiler-explorer/gcc-snapshot -fcolor-diagnostics -fno-crash-diagnostics <source>
1.	<eof> parser at end of file
2.	Optimizer
3.	Running pass "verify" on module "<source>"
```

## Code
```c
struct f {
  float f[1804289383];
};

long double ftest(struct f arg1, long double arg2) { return arg2; }
```

## Code
```c
struct f {
  float f[1804289383];
};

long double ftest(struct f arg1, long double arg2) { return arg2; }
```
</details>


---

### Comment 2 - keinflue

Further reduces to
```cpp
#define N 4294967296 /* any value >= 2**32 */

struct f {
  char f[N];
};

void ftest(struct f arg1) { }
```

Crash is a regression since Clang 19: https://godbolt.org/z/E1vfqKsrr

Note also the `huge 'byval' arguments are unsupported` message in the compiler output.

The title is misleading since there is no flexible array member.

Likely duplicates: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/118207 https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/115655

---
